NKJV.1: The wise woman builds her house, But the foolish pulls it down with her hands.
NKJV.2: He who walks in his uprightness fears the LORD, But he who is perverse in his ways despises Him.
NKJV.3: In the mouth of a fool is a rod of pride, But the lips of the wise will preserve them.
NKJV.4: Where no oxen are, the trough is clean; But much increase comes by the strength of an ox.
NKJV.5: A faithful witness does not lie, But a false witness will utter lies.
NKJV.6: A scoffer seeks wisdom and does not find it, But knowledge is easy to him who understands.
NKJV.7: Go from the presence of a foolish man, When you do not perceive in him the lips of knowledge.
NKJV.8: The wisdom of the prudent is to understand his way, But the folly of fools is deceit.
NKJV.9: Fools mock at sin, But among the upright there is favor.
NKJV.10: The heart knows its own bitterness, And a stranger does not share its joy.
NKJV.11: The house of the wicked will be overthrown, But the tent of the upright will flourish.
NKJV.12: There is a way that seems right to a man, But its end is the way of death.
NKJV.13: Even in laughter the heart may sorrow, And the end of mirth may be grief.
NKJV.14: The backslider in heart will be filled with his own ways, But a good man will be satisfied from above.
NKJV.15: The simple believes every word, But the prudent considers well his steps.
NKJV.16: A wise man fears and departs from evil, But a fool rages and is self-confident.
NKJV.17: A quick-tempered man acts foolishly, And a man of wicked intentions is hated.
NKJV.18: The simple inherit folly, But the prudent are crowned with knowledge.
NKJV.19: The evil will bow before the good, And the wicked at the gates of the righteous.
NKJV.20: The poor man is hated even by his own neighbor, But the rich has many friends.
NKJV.21: He who despises his neighbor sins; But he who has mercy on the poor, happy is he.
NKJV.22: Do they not go astray who devise evil? But mercy and truth belong to those who devise good.
NKJV.23: In all labor there is profit, But idle chatter leads only to poverty.
NKJV.24: The crown of the wise is their riches, But the foolishness of fools is folly.
NKJV.25: A true witness delivers souls, But a deceitful witness speaks lies.
NKJV.26: In the fear of the LORD there is strong confidence, And His children will have a place of refuge.
NKJV.27: The fear of the LORD is a fountain of life, To turn one away from the snares of death.
NKJV.28: In a multitude of people is a king’s honor, But in the lack of people is the downfall of a prince.
NKJV.29: He who is slow to wrath has great understanding, But he who is impulsive exalts folly.
NKJV.30: A sound heart is life to the body, But envy is rottenness to the bones.
NKJV.31: He who oppresses the poor reproaches his Maker, But he who honors Him has mercy on the needy.
NKJV.32: The wicked is banished in his wickedness, But the righteous has a refuge in his death.
NKJV.33: Wisdom rests in the heart of him who has understanding, But what is in the heart of fools is made known.
NKJV.34: Righteousness exalts a nation, But sin is a reproach to any people.
NKJV.35: The king’s favor is toward a wise servant, But his wrath is against him who causes shame.

ESV.1: The wisest of women builds her house, but folly with her own hands tears it down.
ESV.2: Whoever walks in uprightness fears the LORD, but he who is devious in his ways despises him.
ESV.3: By the mouth of a fool comes a rod for his back,  but the lips of the wise will preserve them.
ESV.4: Where there are no oxen, the manger is clean, but abundant crops come by the strength of the ox.
ESV.5: A faithful witness does not lie, but a false witness breathes out lies.
ESV.6: A scoffer seeks wisdom in vain, but knowledge is easy for a man of understanding.
ESV.7: Leave the presence of a fool, for there you do not meet words of knowledge.
ESV.8: The wisdom of the prudent is to discern his way, but the folly of fools is deceiving.
ESV.9: Fools mock at the guilt offering, but the upright enjoy acceptance.
ESV.10: The heart knows its own bitterness, and no stranger shares its joy.
ESV.11: The house of the wicked will be destroyed, but the tent of the upright will flourish.
ESV.12: There is a way that seems right to a man, but its end is the way to death.
ESV.13: Even in laughter the heart may ache, and the end of joy may be grief.
ESV.14: The backslider in heart will be filled with the fruit of his ways, and a good man will be filled with the fruit of his ways.
ESV.15: The simple believes everything, but the prudent gives thought to his steps.
ESV.16: One who is wise is cautious  and turns away from evil, but a fool is reckless and careless.
ESV.17: A man of quick temper acts foolishly, and a man of evil devices is hated.
ESV.18: The simple inherit folly, but the prudent are crowned with knowledge.
ESV.19: The evil bow down before the good, the wicked at the gates of the righteous.
ESV.20: The poor is disliked even by his neighbor, but the rich has many friends.
ESV.21: Whoever despises his neighbor is a sinner, but blessed is he who is generous to the poor.
ESV.22: Do they not go astray who devise evil? Those who devise good meet  steadfast love and faithfulness.
ESV.23: In all toil there is profit, but mere talk tends only to poverty.
ESV.24: The crown of the wise is their wealth, but the folly of fools brings folly.
ESV.25: A truthful witness saves lives, but one who breathes out lies is deceitful.
ESV.26: In the fear of the LORD one has strong confidence, and his children will have a refuge.
ESV.27: The fear of the LORD is a fountain of life, that one may turn away from the snares of death.
ESV.28: In a multitude of people is the glory of a king, but without people a prince is ruined.
ESV.29: Whoever is slow to anger has great understanding, but he who has a hasty temper exalts folly.
ESV.30: A tranquil  heart gives life to the flesh, but envy  makes the bones rot.
ESV.31: Whoever oppresses a poor man insults his Maker, but he who is generous to the needy honors him.
ESV.32: The wicked is overthrown through his evildoing, but the righteous finds refuge in his death.
ESV.33: Wisdom rests in the heart of a man of understanding, but it makes itself known even in the midst of fools.
ESV.34: Righteousness exalts a nation, but sin is a reproach to any people.
ESV.35: A servant who deals wisely has the king's favor, but his wrath falls on one who acts shamefully.

NASB.1: The wise woman builds her house, But the foolish tears it down with her own hands.
NASB.2: He who walks in his uprightness fears the LORD, But he who is devious in his ways despises Him.
NASB.3: In the mouth of the foolish is a rod for [his] back, But the lips of the wise will protect them.
NASB.4: Where no oxen are, the manger is clean, But much revenue [comes] by the strength of the ox.
NASB.5: A trustworthy witness will not lie, But a false witness utters lies.
NASB.6: A scoffer seeks wisdom and [finds] none, But knowledge is easy to one who has understanding.
NASB.7: Leave the presence of a fool, Or you will not discern words of knowledge.
NASB.8: The wisdom of the sensible is to understand his way, But the foolishness of fools is deceit.
NASB.9: Fools mock at sin, But among the upright there is good will.
NASB.10: The heart knows its own bitterness, And a stranger does not share its joy.
NASB.11: The house of the wicked will be destroyed, But the tent of the upright will flourish.
NASB.12: There is a way [which seems] right to a man, But its end is the way of death.
NASB.13: Even in laughter the heart may be in pain, And the end of joy may be grief.
NASB.14: The backslider in heart will have his fill of his own ways, But a good man will [be satisfied] with his.
NASB.15: The naive believes everything, But the sensible man considers his steps.
NASB.16: A wise man is cautious and turns away from evil, But a fool is arrogant and careless.
NASB.17: A quick-tempered man acts foolishly, And a man of evil devices is hated.
NASB.18: The naive inherit foolishness, But the sensible are crowned with knowledge.
NASB.19: The evil will bow down before the good, And the wicked at the gates of the righteous.
NASB.20: The poor is hated even by his neighbor, But those who love the rich are many.
NASB.21: He who despises his neighbor sins, But happy is he who is gracious to the poor.
NASB.22: Will they not go astray who devise evil? But kindness and truth [will be to] those who devise good.
NASB.23: In all labor there is profit, But mere talk [leads] only to poverty.
NASB.24: The crown of the wise is their riches, [But] the folly of fools is foolishness.
NASB.25: A truthful witness saves lives, But he who utters lies is treacherous.
NASB.26: In the fear of the LORD there is strong confidence, And his children will have refuge.
NASB.27: The fear of the LORD is a fountain of life, That one may avoid the snares of death.
NASB.28: In a multitude of people is a king's glory, But in the dearth of people is a prince's ruin.
NASB.29: He who is slow to anger has great understanding, But he who is quick-tempered exalts folly.
NASB.30: A tranquil heart is life to the body, But passion is rottenness to the bones.
NASB.31: He who oppresses the poor taunts his Maker, But he who is gracious to the needy honors Him.
NASB.32: The wicked is thrust down by his wrongdoing, But the righteous has a refuge when he dies.
NASB.33: Wisdom rests in the heart of one who has understanding, But in the hearts of fools it is made known.
NASB.34: Righteousness exalts a nation, But sin is a disgrace to [any] people.
NASB.35: The king's favor is toward a servant who acts wisely, But his anger is toward him who acts shamefully.

NLT.1:
A wise woman builds her home,
but a foolish woman tears it down with her own hands.
NLT.2:
Those who follow the right path fear the LORD;
those who take the wrong path despise him.
NLT.3:
A fool’s proud talk becomes a rod that beats him,
but the words of the wise keep them safe.
NLT.4:
Without oxen a stable stays clean,
but you need a strong ox for a large harvest.
NLT.5:
An honest witness does not lie;
a false witness breathes lies.
NLT.6:
A mocker seeks wisdom and never finds it,
but knowledge comes easily to those with understanding.
NLT.7:
Stay away from fools,
for you won’t find knowledge on their lips.
NLT.8:
The prudent understand where they are going,
but fools deceive themselves.
NLT.9:
Fools make fun of guilt,
but the godly acknowledge it and seek reconciliation.
NLT.10:
Each heart knows its own bitterness,
and no one else can fully share its joy.
NLT.11:
The house of the wicked will be destroyed,
but the tent of the godly will flourish.
NLT.12:
There is a path before each person that seems right,
but it ends in death.
NLT.13:
Laughter can conceal a heavy heart,
but when the laughter ends, the grief remains.
NLT.14:
Backsliders get what they deserve;
good people receive their reward.
NLT.15:
Only simpletons believe everything they’re told!
The prudent carefully consider their steps.
NLT.16:
The wise are cautious and avoid danger;
fools plunge ahead with reckless confidence.
NLT.17:
Short-tempered people do foolish things,
and schemers are hated.
NLT.18:
Simpletons are clothed with foolishness,
but the prudent are crowned with knowledge.
NLT.19:
Evil people will bow before good people;
the wicked will bow at the gates of the godly.
NLT.20:
The poor are despised even by their neighbors,
while the rich have many “friends.”
NLT.21:
It is a sin to belittle one’s neighbor;
blessed are those who help the poor.
NLT.22:
If you plan to do evil, you will be lost;
if you plan to do good, you will receive unfailing love and faithfulness.
NLT.23:
Work brings profit,
but mere talk leads to poverty!
NLT.24:
Wealth is a crown for the wise;
the effort of fools yields only foolishness.
NLT.25:
A truthful witness saves lives,
but a false witness is a traitor.
NLT.26:
Those who fear the LORD are secure;
he will be a refuge for their children.
NLT.27:
Fear of the LORD is a life-giving fountain;
it offers escape from the snares of death.
NLT.28:
A growing population is a king’s glory;
a prince without subjects has nothing.
NLT.29:
People with understanding control their anger;
a hot temper shows great foolishness.
NLT.30:
A peaceful heart leads to a healthy body;
jealousy is like cancer in the bones.
NLT.31:
Those who oppress the poor insult their Maker,
but helping the poor honors him.
NLT.32:
The wicked are crushed by disaster,
but the godly have a refuge when they die.
NLT.33:
Wisdom is enshrined in an understanding heart;
wisdom is not found among fools.
NLT.34:
Godliness makes a nation great,
but sin is a disgrace to any people.
NLT.35:
A king rejoices in wise servants
but is angry with those who disgrace him.

WEB.1: Every wise woman builds her house, but the foolish one tears it down with her own hands.
WEB.2: He who walks in his uprightness fears Yahweh, but he who is perverse in his ways despises him.
WEB.3: The fool’s talk brings a rod to his back, but the lips of the wise protect them.
WEB.4: Where no oxen are, the crib is clean, but much increase is by the strength of the ox.
WEB.5: A truthful witness will not lie, but a false witness pours out lies.
WEB.6: A scoffer seeks wisdom, and doesn’t find it, but knowledge comes easily to a discerning person.
WEB.7: Stay away from a foolish man, for you won’t find knowledge on his lips.
WEB.8: The wisdom of the prudent is to think about his way, but the folly of fools is deceit.
WEB.9: Fools mock at making atonement for sins, but among the upright there is good will.
WEB.10: The heart knows its own bitterness and joy; he will not share these with a stranger.
WEB.11: The house of the wicked will be overthrown, but the tent of the upright will flourish.
WEB.12: There is a way which seems right to a man, but in the end it leads to death.
WEB.13: Even in laughter the heart may be sorrowful, and mirth may end in heaviness.
WEB.14: The unfaithful will be repaid for his own ways; likewise a good man will be rewarded for his ways.
WEB.15: A simple man believes everything, but the prudent man carefully considers his ways.
WEB.16: A wise man fears and shuns evil, but the fool is hot headed and reckless.
WEB.17: He who is quick to become angry will commit folly, and a crafty man is hated.
WEB.18: The simple inherit folly, but the prudent are crowned with knowledge.
WEB.19: The evil bow down before the good, and the wicked at the gates of the righteous.
WEB.20: The poor person is shunned even by his own neighbor, but the rich person has many friends.
WEB.21: He who despises his neighbor sins, but blessed is he who has pity on the poor.
WEB.22: Don’t they go astray who plot evil? But love and faithfulness belong to those who plan good.
WEB.23: In all hard work there is profit, but the talk of the lips leads only to poverty.
WEB.24: The crown of the wise is their riches, but the folly of fools crowns them with folly.
WEB.25: A truthful witness saves souls, but a false witness is deceitful.
WEB.26: In the fear of Yahweh is a secure fortress, and he will be a refuge for his children.
WEB.27: The fear of Yahweh is a fountain of life, turning people from the snares of death.
WEB.28: In the multitude of people is the king’s glory, but in the lack of people is the destruction of the prince.
WEB.29: He who is slow to anger has great understanding, but he who has a quick temper displays folly.
WEB.30: The life of the body is a heart at peace, but envy rots the bones.
WEB.31: He who oppresses the poor shows contempt for his Maker, but he who is kind to the needy honors him.
WEB.32: The wicked is brought down in his calamity, but in death, the righteous has a refuge.
WEB.33: Wisdom rests in the heart of one who has understanding, and is even made known in the inward part of fools.
WEB.34: Righteousness exalts a nation, but sin is a disgrace to any people.
WEB.35: The king’s favor is toward a servant who deals wisely, but his wrath is toward one who causes shame.

CSB17.1:

but a foolish one tears it down with her own hands. ⓔ
CSB17.2:

but the one who is devious in his ways despises him. ⓖ
CSB17.3:
The proud speech of a fool brings a rod of discipline, [1]
but the lips of the wise protect them. ⓗ
CSB17.4:
Where there are no oxen, the feeding trough is empty, [2]
but an abundant harvest comes through the strength of an ox.
CSB17.5:
An honest witness does not deceive,
but a dishonest witness utters lies. ⓘ
CSB17.6:
A mocker seeks wisdom and doesn’t find it,
but knowledge comes easily to the perceptive.
CSB17.7:
Stay away from a foolish person;
you will gain no knowledge from his speech. ⓙ
CSB17.8:
The sensible person’s wisdom is to consider his way,
but the stupidity of fools deceives them.
CSB17.9:
Fools mock at making reparation, [3]
but there is goodwill among the upright.
CSB17.10:
The heart knows its own bitterness,
and no outsider shares in its joy.
CSB17.11:
The house of the wicked will be destroyed,
but the tent of the upright will flourish. ⓚ
CSB17.12:
There is a way that seems right to a person, ⓛ
but its end is the way to death. ⓜ
CSB17.13:
Even in laughter a heart may be sad,
and joy may end in grief. ⓝ
CSB17.14:
The disloyal one will get what his conduct deserves,
and a good one, what his deeds deserve. ⓐ
CSB17.15:
The inexperienced one believes anything, ⓑ
but the sensible one watches [4] his steps.
CSB17.16:
A wise person is cautious and turns from evil, ⓒ
but a fool is easily angered and is careless. [5]
CSB17.17:
A quick-tempered person acts foolishly,
and one who schemes is hated. ⓓ
CSB17.18:
The inexperienced inherit foolishness,
but the sensible are crowned with knowledge.
CSB17.19:
The evil bow before those who are good,
and the wicked, at the gates of the righteous.
CSB17.20:
A poor person is hated even by his neighbor, ⓔ
but there are many who love the rich. ⓕ
CSB17.21:
The one who despises his neighbor sins, ⓖ
but whoever shows kindness to the poor will be happy. ⓗ
CSB17.22:
Don’t those who plan evil go astray? ⓘ
But those who plan good find loyalty and faithfulness. ⓙ
CSB17.23:
There is profit in all hard work,
but endless talk [6] leads only to poverty. ⓚ
CSB17.24:
The crown of the wise is their wealth,
but the foolishness of fools produces foolishness.
CSB17.25:
A truthful witness rescues lives,
but one who utters lies is deceitful. ⓛ
CSB17.26:
In the fear of the LORD one has strong confidence
and his children have a refuge. ⓜ
CSB17.27:
The fear of the LORD is a fountain of life,
turning people away from the snares of death. ⓝ
CSB17.28:
A large population is a king’s splendor,
but a shortage of people is a ruler’s devastation.
CSB17.29:
A patient person shows great understanding, ⓐ
but a quick-tempered one promotes foolishness.
CSB17.30:
A tranquil heart is life to the body, ⓑ
but jealousy is rottenness to the bones. ⓒ
CSB17.31:
The one who oppresses the poor person insults his Maker, ⓓ
but one who is kind to the needy honors him. ⓔ
CSB17.32:
The wicked one is thrown down by his own sin, ⓕ
but the righteous one has a refuge in his death. ⓖ
CSB17.33:
Wisdom resides in the heart of the discerning; ⓗ
she is known [7] even among fools.
CSB17.34:
Righteousness exalts a nation,
but sin is a disgrace to any people. ⓘ
CSB17.35:
A king favors a prudent servant, ⓙ
but his anger falls on a disgraceful one.

AMP.1:
The wise woman builds her house [on a foundation of godly precepts, and her household thrives],
But the foolish one [who lacks spiritual insight] tears it down with her own hands [by ignoring godly principles].
AMP.2:
He who walks in uprightness [reverently] fears the LORD [and obeys and worships Him with profound respect],
But he who is devious in his ways despises Him.
AMP.3:
In the mouth of the [arrogant] fool [who rejects God] is a rod for his back,
But the lips of the wise [when they speak with godly wisdom] will protect them.
AMP.4:
Where there are no oxen, the manger is clean,
But much revenue [because of good crops] comes by the strength of the ox.
AMP.5:
A faithful and trustworthy witness will not lie,
But a false witness speaks lies.
AMP.6:
A scoffer seeks wisdom and finds none [for his ears are closed to wisdom],
But knowledge is easy for one who understands [because he is willing to learn].
AMP.7:
Leave the presence of a [shortsighted] fool,
For you will not find knowledge or hear godly wisdom from his lips.
AMP.8:
The wisdom of the sensible is to understand his way,
But the foolishness of [shortsighted] fools is deceit.
AMP.9:
Fools mock sin [but sin mocks the fools],
But among the upright there is good will and the favor and blessing of God.
AMP.10:
The heart knows its own bitterness,
And no stranger shares its joy.
AMP.11:
The house of the wicked will be overthrown,
But the tent of the upright will thrive.
AMP.12:
There is a way which seems right to a man and appears straight before him,
But its end is the way of death.
AMP.13:
Even in laughter the heart may be in pain,
And the end of joy may be grief.
AMP.14:
The backslider in heart will have his fill with his own [rotten] ways,
But a good man will be satisfied with his ways [the godly thought and action which his heart pursues and in which he delights].
AMP.15:
The naive or inexperienced person [is easily misled and] believes every word he hears,
But the prudent man [is discreet and astute and] considers well where he is going.
AMP.16:
A wise man suspects danger and cautiously avoids evil,
But the fool is arrogant and careless.
AMP.17:
A quick-tempered man acts foolishly and without self-control,
And a man of wicked schemes is hated.
AMP.18:
The naive [are unsophisticated and easy to exploit and] inherit foolishness,
But the sensible [are thoughtful and far-sighted and] are crowned with knowledge.
AMP.19:
The evil will bow down before the good,
And the wicked [will bow down] at the gates of the righteous.
AMP.20:
The poor man is hated even by his neighbor,
But those who love the rich are many.
AMP.21:
He who despises his neighbor sins [against God and his fellow man],
But happy [blessed and favored by God] is he who is gracious and merciful to the poor.
AMP.22:
Do they not go astray who devise evil and wander from the way of righteousness?
But kindness and truth will be to those who devise good.
AMP.23:
In all labor there is profit,
But mere talk leads only to poverty.
AMP.24:
The crown of the wise is their wealth [of wisdom],
But the foolishness of [closed-minded] fools is [nothing but] folly.
AMP.25:
A truthful witness saves lives,
But he who speaks lies is treacherous.
AMP.26:
In the [reverent] fear of the LORD there is strong confidence,
And His children will [always] have a place of refuge.
AMP.27:
The [reverent] fear of the LORD [that leads to obedience and worship] is a fountain of life,
So that one may avoid the snares of death.
AMP.28:
In a multitude of people is a king's glory,
But in a lack of people is a [pretentious] prince's ruin.
AMP.29:
He who is slow to anger has great understanding [and profits from his self-control],
But he who is quick-tempered exposes and exalts his foolishness [for all to see].
AMP.30:
A calm and peaceful and tranquil heart is life and health to the body,
But passion and envy are like rottenness to the bones.
AMP.31:
He who oppresses the poor taunts and insults his Maker,
But he who is kind and merciful and gracious to the needy honors Him.
AMP.32:
The wicked is overthrown through his wrongdoing,
But the righteous has hope and confidence and a refuge [with God] even in death.
AMP.33:
Wisdom rests [silently] in the heart of one who has understanding,
But what is in the heart of [shortsighted] fools is made known.
AMP.34:
Righteousness [moral and spiritual integrity and virtuous character] exalts a nation,
But sin is a disgrace to any people.
AMP.35:
The king's favor and good will are toward a servant who acts wisely and discreetly,
But his anger and wrath are toward him who acts shamefully.

MSG.1:
Lady Wisdom builds a lovely home;
Sir Fool comes along and tears it down brick by brick.
MSG.2:
An honest life shows respect for God;
a degenerate life is a slap in his face.
MSG.3:
Frivolous talk provokes a derisive smile;
wise speech evokes nothing but respect.
MSG.4:
No cattle, no crops;
a good harvest requires a strong ox for the plow.
MSG.5:
A true witness never lies;
a false witness makes a business of it.
MSG.6:
Cynics look high and low for wisdom—and never find it;
the open-minded find it right on their doorstep!
MSG.7:
Escape quickly from the company of fools;
they're a waste of your time, a waste of your words.
MSG.8:
The wisdom of the wise keeps life on track;
the foolishness of fools lands them in the ditch.
MSG.9:
The stupid ridicule right and wrong,
but a moral life is a favored life.
MSG.10:
The person who shuns the bitter moments of friends
will be an outsider at their celebrations.
MSG.11:
Lives of careless wrongdoing are tumbledown shacks;
holy living builds soaring cathedrals.
MSG.12:
There's a way of life that looks harmless enough;
look again—it leads straight to hell.
MSG.13:
Sure, those people appear to be having a good time,
but all that laughter will end in heartbreak.
MSG.14:
A mean person gets paid back in meanness,
a gracious person in grace.
MSG.15:
The gullible believe anything they're told;
the prudent sift and weigh every word.
MSG.16:
The wise watch their steps and avoid evil;
fools are headstrong and reckless.
MSG.17:
The hotheaded do things they'll later regret;
the coldhearted get the cold shoulder.
MSG.18:
Foolish dreamers live in a world of illusion;
wise realists plant their feet on the ground.
MSG.19:
Eventually, evil will pay tribute to good;
the wicked will respect God-loyal people.
MSG.20:
An unlucky loser is shunned by all,
but everyone loves a winner.
MSG.21:
It's criminal to ignore a neighbor in need,
but compassion for the poor—what a blessing!
MSG.22:
Isn't it obvious that conspirators lose out,
while the thoughtful win love and trust?
MSG.23:
Hard work always pays off;
mere talk puts no bread on the table.
MSG.24:
The wise accumulate wisdom;
fools get stupider by the day.
MSG.25:
Souls are saved by truthful witness
and betrayed by the spread of lies.
MSG.26:
The Fear-of-God builds up confidence,
and makes a world safe for your children.
MSG.27:
The Fear-of-God is a spring of living water
so you won't go off drinking from poisoned wells.
MSG.28:
The mark of a good leader is loyal followers;
leadership is nothing without a following.
MSG.29:
Slowness to anger makes for deep understanding;
a quick-tempered person stockpiles stupidity.
MSG.30:
A sound mind makes for a robust body,
but runaway emotions corrode the bones.
MSG.31:
You insult your Maker when you exploit the powerless;
when you're kind to the poor, you honor God.
MSG.32:
The evil of bad people leaves them out in the cold;
the integrity of good people creates a safe place for living.
MSG.33:
Lady Wisdom is at home in an understanding heart—
fools never even get to say hello.
MSG.34:
God-devotion makes a country strong;
God-avoidance leaves people weak.
MSG.35:
Diligent work gets a warm commendation;
shiftless work earns an angry rebuke.

NIV.1:
The wise woman builds her house,
but with her own hands the foolish one tears hers down.
NIV.2:
He whose walk is upright fears the Lord,
but he whose ways are devious despises him.
NIV.3:
A fool’s talk brings a rod to his back,
but the lips of the wise protect them.
NIV.4:
Where there are no oxen, the manger is empty,
but from the strength of an ox comes an abundant harvest.
NIV.5:
A truthful witness does not deceive,
but a false witness pours out lies.
NIV.6:
The mocker seeks wisdom and finds none,
but knowledge comes easily to the discerning.
NIV.7:
Stay away from a foolish man,
for you will not find knowledge on his lips.
NIV.8:
The wisdom of the prudent is to give thought to their ways,
but the folly of fools is deception.
NIV.9:
Fools mock at making amends for sin,
but goodwill is found among the upright.
NIV.10:
Each heart knows its own bitterness,
and no one else can share its joy.
NIV.11:
The house of the wicked will be destroyed,
but the tent of the upright will flourish.
NIV.12:
There is a way that seems right to a man,
but in the end it leads to death.
NIV.13:
Even in laughter the heart may ache,
and joy may end in grief.
NIV.14:
The faithless will be fully repaid for their ways,
and the good man rewarded for his.
NIV.15:
A simple man believes anything,
but a prudent man gives thought to his steps.
NIV.16:
A wise man fears the Lord and shuns evil,
but a fool is hotheaded and reckless.
NIV.17:
A quick-tempered man does foolish things,
and a crafty man is hated.
NIV.18:
The simple inherit folly,
but the prudent are crowned with knowledge.
NIV.19:
Evil men will bow down in the presence of the good,
and the wicked at the gates of the righteous.
NIV.20:
The poor are shunned even by their neighbors,
but the rich have many friends.
NIV.21:
He who despises his neighbor sins,
but blessed is he who is kind to the needy.
NIV.22:
Do not those who plot evil go astray?
But those who plan what is good find love and faithfulness.
NIV.23:
All hard work brings a profit,
but mere talk leads only to poverty.
NIV.24:
The wealth of the wise is their crown,
but the folly of fools yields folly.
NIV.25:
A truthful witness saves lives,
but a false witness is deceitful.
NIV.26:
He who fears the Lord has a secure fortress,
and for his children it will be a refuge.
NIV.27:
The fear of the Lord is a fountain of life,
turning a man from the snares of death.
NIV.28:
A large population is a king’s glory,
but without subjects a prince is ruined.
NIV.29:
A patient man has great understanding,
but a quick-tempered man displays folly.
NIV.30:
A heart at peace gives life to the body,
but envy rots the bones.
NIV.31:
He who oppresses the poor shows contempt for their Maker,
but whoever is kind to the needy honors God.
NIV.32:
When calamity comes, the wicked are brought down,
but even in death the righteous have a refuge.
NIV.33:
Wisdom reposes in the heart of the discerning
and even among fools she lets herself be known.
NIV.34:
Righteousness exalts a nation,
but sin is a disgrace to any people.
NIV.35:
A king delights in a wise servant,
but a shameful servant incurs his wrath.

NRSVCE.1:
The wise woman builds her house,
but the foolish tears it down with her own hands.
NRSVCE.2:
Those who walk uprightly fear the LORD,
but one who is devious in conduct despises him.
NRSVCE.3:
The talk of fools is a rod for their backs,
but the lips of the wise preserve them.
NRSVCE.4:
Where there are no oxen, there is no grain;
abundant crops come by the strength of the ox.
NRSVCE.5:
A faithful witness does not lie,
but a false witness breathes out lies.
NRSVCE.6:
A scoffer seeks wisdom in vain,
but knowledge is easy for one who understands.
NRSVCE.7:
Leave the presence of a fool,
for there you do not find words of knowledge.
NRSVCE.8:
It is the wisdom of the clever to understand where they go,
but the folly of fools misleads.
NRSVCE.9:
Fools mock at the guilt offering,
but the upright enjoy God’s favor.
NRSVCE.10:
The heart knows its own bitterness,
and no stranger shares its joy.
NRSVCE.11:
The house of the wicked is destroyed,
but the tent of the upright flourishes.
NRSVCE.12:
There is a way that seems right to a person,
but its end is the way to death.
NRSVCE.13:
Even in laughter the heart is sad,
and the end of joy is grief.
NRSVCE.14:
The perverse get what their ways deserve,
and the good, what their deeds deserve.
NRSVCE.15:
The simple believe everything,
but the clever consider their steps.
NRSVCE.16:
The wise are cautious and turn away from evil,
but the fool throws off restraint and is careless.
NRSVCE.17:
One who is quick-tempered acts foolishly,
and the schemer is hated.
NRSVCE.18:
The simple are adorned with folly,
but the clever are crowned with knowledge.
NRSVCE.19:
The evil bow down before the good,
the wicked at the gates of the righteous.
NRSVCE.20:
The poor are disliked even by their neighbors,
but the rich have many friends.
NRSVCE.21:
Those who despise their neighbors are sinners,
but happy are those who are kind to the poor.
NRSVCE.22:
Do they not err that plan evil?
Those who plan good find loyalty and faithfulness.
NRSVCE.23:
In all toil there is profit,
but mere talk leads only to poverty.
NRSVCE.24:
The crown of the wise is their wisdom,
but folly is the garland of fools.
NRSVCE.25:
A truthful witness saves lives,
but one who utters lies is a betrayer.
NRSVCE.26:
In the fear of the LORD one has strong confidence,
and one’s children will have a refuge.
NRSVCE.27:
The fear of the LORD is a fountain of life,
so that one may avoid the snares of death.
NRSVCE.28:
The glory of a king is a multitude of people;
without people a prince is ruined.
NRSVCE.29:
Whoever is slow to anger has great understanding,
but one who has a hasty temper exalts folly.
NRSVCE.30:
A tranquil mind gives life to the flesh,
but passion makes the bones rot.
NRSVCE.31:
Those who oppress the poor insult their Maker,
but those who are kind to the needy honor him.
NRSVCE.32:
The wicked are overthrown by their evildoing,
but the righteous find a refuge in their integrity.
NRSVCE.33:
Wisdom is at home in the mind of one who has understanding,
but it is not known in the heart of fools.
NRSVCE.34:
Righteousness exalts a nation,
but sin is a reproach to any people.
NRSVCE.35:
A servant who deals wisely has the king’s favor,
but his wrath falls on one who acts shamefully.

NET.1: Every wise woman builds her household, but a foolish woman tears it down with her own hands.
NET.2: The one who walks in his uprightness fears the LORD, but the one who is perverted in his ways despises him.
NET.3: In the speech of a fool is a rod for his back, but the words of the wise protect them.
NET.4: Where there are no oxen, the feeding trough is clean, but an abundant harvest is produced by strong oxen.
NET.5: A truthful witness does not lie, but a false witness breathes out lies.
NET.6: The scorner seeks wisdom but finds none, but understanding is easy for a discerning person.
NET.7: Leave the presence of a foolish person, or you will not understand wise counsel.
NET.8: The wisdom of the shrewd person is to discern his way, but the folly of fools is deception.
NET.9: Fools mock at reparation, but among the upright there is favor.
NET.10: The heart knows its own bitterness, and with its joy no one else can share.
NET.11: The household of the wicked will be destroyed, but the tent of the upright will flourish.
NET.12: There is a way that seems right to a person, but its end is the way that leads to death.
NET.13: Even in laughter the heart may ache, and the end of joy may be grief.
NET.14: The backslider will be paid back from his own ways, but a good person will be rewarded for his.
NET.15: A naive person believes everything, but the shrewd person discerns his steps.
NET.16: A wise person is cautious and turns from evil, but a fool throws off restraint and is overconfident.
NET.17: A person who has a quick temper does foolish things, and a person with crafty schemes is hated.
NET.18: The naive inherit folly, but the shrewd are crowned with knowledge.
NET.19: Those who are evil will bow before those who are good, and the wicked will bow at the gates of the righteous.
NET.20: A poor person is disliked even by his neighbors, but those who love the rich are many.
NET.21: The one who despises his neighbor sins, but whoever is kind to the needy is blessed.
NET.22: Do not those who devise evil go astray? But those who plan good exhibit faithful covenant love.
NET.23: In all hard work there is profit, but merely talking about it only brings poverty.
NET.24: The crown of the wise is their riches, but the folly of fools is folly.
NET.25: A truthful witness rescues lives, but the one who breathes lies brings deception.
NET.26: In the fear of the LORD one has strong confidence, and it will be a refuge for his children.
NET.27: The fear of the LORD is like a life-giving fountain, to turn people from deadly snares.
NET.28: A king’s glory is the abundance of people, but the lack of subjects is the ruin of a ruler.
NET.29: The one who is slow to anger has great understanding, but the one who has a quick temper exalts folly.
NET.30: A tranquil spirit revives the body, but envy is rottenness to the bones.
NET.31: The one who oppresses the poor insults his Creator, but whoever shows favor to the needy honors him.
NET.32: The wicked will be thrown down in his trouble, but the righteous have refuge even in the threat of death.
NET.33: Wisdom rests in the heart of the discerning; it is known even in the heart of fools.
NET.34: Righteousness exalts a nation, but sin is a disgrace to any people.
NET.35: The king shows favor to a wise servant, but his wrath falls on one who acts shamefully.
